channel.title: Schwalbe on RaihnForge
channel.description: Recent content in Schwalbe on RaihnForge

item.title: Final Individual Reflection: Team Project Performance
item.description:
This is the end-of-quarter reflection for ADMG 374. The team delivered a nonprofit website project across three labs and a final, with four members working entirely over Microsoft Teams and Zoom during the COVID quarter. The team average came out at 99.7. I scored the team at 96, reserving room for improvement that any team can use.

Team Performance

Teammate 1 was excellent at the work itself, and was open about a schedule pulling them in many directions. Their risk management section in Lab 3 carried the depth of someone who had taken a dedicated risk class the prior quarter, and it raised the ceiling of the deliverable.

item.title: Communicating About and Managing Risk
item.description:
This paper responds to Schwalbe Chapters 10 and 11. Chapter 10 covers communication. Chapter 11 covers risk. They are paired for a reason: most of what we call risk in projects is, underneath, a communication failure that nobody named in time.

Chapter 10: Communication

Tone of Voice

Oral communication carries information that words alone do not (Schwalbe, 2015, p. 394). Someone can say “I am fine with the timeline” and be telling you something different with their voice. Stress and tension show up in tone before they show up in vocabulary, often before the speaker has even registered the feeling themselves. A project manager who only reads the words is reading half the message.
